nonBlockingIssues.description: Location token matching in buildRetryLadder is word-based. Multi-word locations like 'United States' are split into tokens so rung 1 (drop location) only removes single-word locations like 'Remote'.

HTTP errors return empty candidates without throwing, allowing retry ladder to continue (fail-open resilience). Caller doesn't distinguish between 'zero candidates because query found nothing' vs 'zero candidates because query failed'.
zero_results diagnostic not emitted when seedCandidates is empty (edge case not covered by tests).
Timeout error handler uses fetch_fallback diagnostic code. A dedicated 'timeout' code might be clearer, but current choice is acceptable as fallback behavior.
No test coverage for timeout handler path in runGroundedWebDiscovery.
No test coverage for low_content_html diagnostic path specifically.
regexFallbackUsed flag is set even when regex fallback returns zero candidates (fallback was attempted, not that it succeeded). Slightly misleading but matches expected behavior per tests.
Test expectation for employer bonus ranking appears potentially inconsistent with code-level scoring trace, but tests pass.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update VAL-OBS-001, VAL-OBS-003, VAL-CROSS-002 assertions from 'pending' to 'passed' since implementation satisfies the contracts and tests pass.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature ultraplan-extraction-fallback-and-zero-result-diagnostics (commit f0bdcc9) implements extraction diagnostic system with stable codes covering VAL-OBS-001 (fetch_fallback, low_content_spa/skeleton), VAL-OBS-003 (zero_results diagnostic), and VAL-CROSS-002 (paired diagnostics + warning strings). Tests pass per handoff verification.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add example of extraction fallback diagnostic pattern (regexFallbackUsed field + warning string) as implementation reference for future workers.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature ultraplan-regex-recovery-denylist-and-employer-ranking correctly implements AGENTS.md guidance item #7 but provides no example. Code review flagged this as a pattern worth documenting.
